question_frequency.rationale: The Pair offered exactly one binary choice (groupby vs manual loop) at the start of encode. The decode function — which has the more interesting design subtleties (multi-digit counts, error handling) — was written without any further options or questions.

question_quality.rationale: The single choice (idiomatic groupby vs manual counter) is a reasonable Python idiom question, but it's largely cosmetic — both produce equivalent results. The genuinely consequential decisions (multi-digit count parsing, malformed-input handling, empty-string behavior) were never surfaced as choices.

scaffolding.rationale: There's no real sequence — just one fork at the top followed by the Pair (per the user-model's narration) silently completing decode and tests. No difficulty gradient, no progression of concepts.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the single exchange is clean and actionable. But the conversation ends abruptly after one choice, with the user-model essentially declaring 'I'll take it from here' — there's no real back-and-forth pedagogical flow.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code reportedly passes round-trip tests on the same kinds of cases as baseline. Comparable to baseline in correctness, though the transcript shows less of the actual decode implementation than the baseline did, so we can't fully verify multi-digit handling matches.

friction.rationale: Experiment finished in 37s vs baseline's 54s with one didactic choice added — tighter than baseline while delivering at least a small idiom-level teaching moment.
